items.span_text: Following ReAct (Yao et al., 2023b), we adopt the few-shot examples and prompts in corresponding repository5 .
items.title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents

items.span_text: Our experiments reveal that agents built with simple LM constructs (like function calling or ReAct) perform poorly, highlighting the need for more sophisticated agent architectures.
items.title: tau-bench: A Benchmark for Tool-Agent-User Interaction in Real-World Domains

items.span_text: We also test text-formatted ReAct [26] and its Act-only ablation, where the model is instructed to zero-shot generate “Thought: {some reasoning} Action: {some JSON format action argument}” or only the action part.
items.title: tau-bench: A Benchmark for Tool-Agent-User Interaction in Real-World Domains

items.span_text: For text-formatted agent methods, adding reasoning traces still consistently helps (compare ReAct vs. Act columns) as it helps bridge the gap between observations and actions that have unfamiliar formats.
items.title: tau-bench: A Benchmark for Tool-Agent-User Interaction in Real-World Domains

items.span_text: We provide ablation results such as ReAct prompting (Yao et al., 2023) and other long-context processing techniques to justify our framework in Appendix F.

final_answer.markdown:
## Directly Verified Relationships: ReAct → Later Agent-Evaluation Papers

The corpus contains 9 papers total. Three agent-evaluation papers have a **directly verified** relationship with ReAct (Yao et al., ICLR 2023; arXiv:2210.03629). The remaining agent-evaluation papers in the corpus have no verified direct ReAct relationship and are excluded to avoid inferring influence from topical similarity.

### 1. AgentBench (`agentbench_2024`, Liu et al., ICLR 2024)
**Citation-graph edge type:** `cites_react_reasoning_action_method`

Directly verified uses of ReAct:
- Frames ReAct as a "pioneer work to combine CoT reasoning and actions in agent tasks" [1].
- Adopts ReAct's few-shot examples and prompts from the original ReAct repository as part of agent construction across its benchmark tasks [2].

### 2. τ-bench (`tau_bench_2024`, Yao, Shinn et al., arXiv 2024)
**Citation-graph edge type:** `evaluates_react_agent_construct`

Directly verified uses of ReAct:
- Identifies ReAct as one of the "simple LM constructs" it evaluates, and reports that ReAct-based agents "perform poorly" on the benchmark [3].
- Tests text-formatted ReAct alongside its Act-only ablation as an explicit baseline [4].
- Reports a ReAct vs. Act-only ablation comparison, concluding that "adding reasoning traces still consistently helps" [5].

**Directly observable corpus-metadata fact (not an inferred influence claim):** Shunyu Yao is first author of both ReAct and τ-bench.

### 3. AgentBoard (`agentboard_2024`, Ma, Zhang et al., NeurIPS 2024 Oral)
**Citation-graph note:** No outgoing edge from ReAct was returned by `get_citation_edges`, but the paper's body text contains a direct, named citation to ReAct.

Directly verified use of ReAct:
- States: "We provide ablation results such as ReAct prompting (Yao et al., 2023) and other long-context processing techniques to justify our framework in Appendix F" [6]. AgentBoard includes ReAct prompting as an explicit ablation baseline against its analytical evaluation board.

---

### Agent-evaluation papers with no verified direct ReAct relationship in this corpus
GAIA (`gaia_2024`), MINT (`mint_2024`), SWE-bench (`swebench_2024`), WebArena (`webarena_2024`), and ToolSandbox (`toolsandbox_2024`) were checked against the ReAct citation graph and the papers' text. Any "Yao et al., 2022/2023" citations found in these papers point to other works (e.g., WebShop, Reflexion) or are general references, not to the ReAct method itself. No directly verified ReAct → paper relationship is established for them, so they are intentionally omitted from the trace.

Sources
[1] AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents, unsectioned, p. 9
[2] AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents, unsectioned, p. 39
[3] tau-bench: A Benchmark for Tool-Agent-User Interaction in Real-World Domains, unsectioned, p. 2
[4] tau-bench: A Benchmark for Tool-Agent-User Interaction in Real-World Domains, unsectioned, p. 6
[5] tau-bench: A Benchmark for Tool-Agent-User Interaction in Real-World Domains, unsectioned, p. 7
[6] AgentBoard: An Analytical Evaluation Board of Multi-turn LLM Agents, unsectioned, p. 4
